data.title: Hold Larsen and Toubro Technology Services; target of Rs 3380: ICICI Securities
data.description:
ICICI Securities research report on Larsen and Toubro Technology Services

LTTS reported a miss on revenue in Q4FY26, given the impact from its divestment of the SWC business. Takeaways: 1) Automotive is stabilising (in line with TELX’s commentary). 2) Continued positive traction in sustainability business (with broad uptick across subsegments). 3) 13–15% revenue CAGR till FY31 for the continuing business under Lakshya 31 program. 4) Completion of restructuring exercise as of Q4FY26. We maintain our neutral stance on LTTS, as execution remains paramount amid a slew of strategy and portfolio changes. While management’s robust 13–15% revenue CAGR outlook gives confidence, we estimate that FY27 revenue growth will likely be in mid-single-digits amid the strategic pivot, leadership changes and macro uncertainty.

Outlook

We maintain our HOLD rating based on an unchanged one-year forward P/E multiple of 22x with a Mar’27E TP of INR 3,380. We cut our FY27–28E EPS by 5-6% to factor in weaker estimated growth in the hi-tech vertical.

Disclaimer

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

LTTS- 2304026 - icici

data.title: Norway's DNB Bank Posts Softer Profit
data.description:
(05:31 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Annualized Return on Equity 14.0%
(05:31 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Annualized Return on Equity 13.6%
(05:31 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q CET1 Ratio 18.1%

(05:32 GMT) *DNB Bank 1Q Net Interest Income NOK15.30B

(05:33 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Net Commissions, Fees NOK4.13B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Net Commissions, Fees NOK4.27B
(05:33 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Rev NOK21.79B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Rev NOK21.78B
(05:33 GMT) *DnB Bank 1Q Net Pft NOK9.86B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw DnB Bank 1Q Net Pft NOK9.20B
(06:08 GMT) Norway's DNB Bank Posts Softer Profit

By Elena Vardon

Norway's DNB Bank reported a fall in first-quarter profit on higher costs along with a mixed top-line performance.

The lender on Thursday posted 9.86 billion kroner ($1.06 billion) in profit attributable to shareholders for the three-month period, below the 10.85 billion kroner it made in the same period in the prior year.

Total income slipped to 21.79 billion kroner from 21.91 billion kroner, on softer net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and what they pay out on client deposits. The bank noted this was due to repricing, adding that there is also great competition for customers and low activity in housing development.

This was somewhat cushioned by a rise in revenue from fees and commissions for the quarter as DNB benefited from client demand for advice and interest in savings and investment products.

Costs rose nearly 7% for the quarter due to the inclusion of Carnegie, the Swedish investment bank it acquired last year, it added.

"The Norwegian economy has nevertheless proved to be highly resilient during periods of turmoil, and developments in the first quarter confirm this," Chief Executive Kjerstin Braathen said.

DNB's common equity Tier 1 capital ratio--a measure of a bank's financial strength--stood at 18.1%, roughly in line with expectations.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Oxford Metrics HY revenue rises slightly; keeps FY view
data.description:
Overview

  • UK smart sensing firm estimates half-year revenue rose slightly yr/yr to £20.7 mln

  • Underlying margins remained robust, with improved adjusted LBIT vs prior year period

  • Macroeconomic uncertainty led some Smart Manufacturing customers to defer project starts

Outlook

  • Oxford Metrics maintains FY26 management expectations for the 15-month period ending Dec 2026

  • Company expects efficiency and margin benefits from division integration to build through FY26

  • Oxford Metrics says macroeconomic uncertainty affecting order timing but pipeline supports FY26 delivery

Result Drivers

  • INTERNATIONAL CONTRACT WINS - Motion Capture division (Vicon) achieved revenue growth due to significant contract wins in Eastern Europe, Japan and India

  • DIVISIONAL INTEGRATION - Smart Manufacturing division unified as Industrial Vision and Metrology Systems, expected to improve efficiency and margins over time

  • MACRO UNCERTAINTY - Heightened macroeconomic uncertainty led some Smart Manufacturing customers to defer project starts, affecting order timing

data.title: Estonia's Tallink Q1 revenue rises on passenger and cargo growth
data.description:
Overview

  • Estonia ferry and cruise operator's Q1 revenue rose 9% yr/yr

  • Q1 EBITDA turned positive, net loss narrowed compared to prior year

  • Company plans to propose EUR 0.06 per share dividend for 2026

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific financial guidance for the current or future periods in its press release

Result Drivers

  • PASSENGER AND CARGO GROWTH - Higher passenger numbers (+6.8% yr/yr) and cargo units (+13.4% yr/yr) boosted revenue

  • WEAK DEMAND AND HIGHER COSTS - Ongoing economic challenges, weak demand, higher fuel and emission costs weighed on results

  • VESSEL MAINTENANCE - Scheduled maintenance reduced available capacity, affecting passenger and cargo levels

data.title: European Shares Poised To Open Lower As Investors Weigh Energy Risks
data.description:
BRUSSELS/FRANKFURT/PARIS (dpa-AFX) - European stocks are set for a lower opening on Thursday as inflation and growth concerns mount.

Iran fired on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz and seized two of them for 'maritime violations', escalating tensions in the region.

The incident underscored the fragility of the ceasefire and pushed Brent crude prices to around $104 a barrel, reigniting worries about inflation, interest rates and economic growth.

Tehran said it remains open for negotiations, but the Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to 'the blatant violations of the ceasefire' by the U.S. and Israel.

In a post on X, Iran's chief negotiator said the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports amounted to taking the global economy 'hostage' and 'warmongering' by Israel 'on all fronts.'

U.S. President Trump also signaled a firm stance, saying there can be no compromise on 'sanity and common sense' in dealing with Iran.

U.S. stock index futures edged lower, a day after Wall Street's main indexes surged to record highs.

Tesla stock remained under selling pressure in extended trading after the electric vehicle maker posted mixed results.

Honeywell International, American Express, Blackstone, American Airlines and Lockheed Martin Corporation are among the prominent companies scheduled to unveil their financial results later in the day.

Asian markets were broadly lower amid mounting geopolitical uncertainty and growing risk aversion to financial assets as the U.S.-Iran remained locked over the control of the Strait of Hormuz.

The dollar wobbled near a 1-1/2-week high and gold fell toward $4,700 an ounce while oil prices extended gains for the fourth straight session after EIA data revealed declines in U.S. inventories across key refined products.

U.S. stocks rallied overnight as strong earnings outweighed lingering tensions around Iran and the Strait of Hormuz.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite surged 1.6 percent and the S&P 500 added 1.1 percent to reach new record closing highs as President Trump extended the U.S. ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks but maintained an American blockade of Iranian ports. The narrower Dow advanced 0.7 percent.

European stocks ended lower for a third straight session on Wednesday as uncertainty persisted over U.S.-Iran negotiations, and Germany cut its growth forecasts for 2026 and 2027.

The pan European Stoxx 600 dropped 0.4 percent. The German DAX dipped 0.3 percent, France's CAC 40 shed 1 percent and the U.K.'s FTSE 100 eased 0.2 percent.

data.title: Sanofi Posts Higher Sales Amid Leadership Transition
data.description:
(05:31 GMT) *Sanofi 1Q Net Sales EUR10.51B
(05:32 GMT) *Sanofi 1Q Net Sales Rose 14% on Year at Constant Exchange Rates
(05:32 GMT) *Analysts Saw Sanofi 1Q Net Sales at EUR10.22B
(05:33 GMT) *Sanofi 1Q Business Oper Pft EUR2.97B
(05:33 GMT) *Analysts Saw Sanofi 1Q Business Oper Pft at EUR2.85B
(05:33 GMT) *Sanofi 1Q Business Net Pft EUR2.26B
(05:34 GMT) *Sanofi Backs 2026 View
(05:34 GMT) *Sanofi Sees Sales Growth by a High Single-Digit Percentage in 2026
(05:34 GMT) *Sanofi Sees Business EPS Growth Slightly Faster Than Sales in 2026
(05:34 GMT) *Sanofi: 2026 Expectations Are at Constant Exchange Rates
(05:57 GMT) Sanofi Posts Higher Sales Amid Leadership Transition

By Andrea Figueras

France's Sanofi booked an increase in sales for the first quarter, boosted by new launches and recent acquisitions at a time when the group undergoes a leadership change.

The drugmaker on Thursday reported net sales of 10.51 billion euros ($12.30 billion) for the period, up around 14% on year at constant currency. Business operating profit--one of the company's preferred metrics--jumped nearly 11% to 2.97 billion euros compared with the prior-year period.

Analysts had expected 10.22 billion euros in sales and business operating profit of 2.85 billion euros for the quarter, according to Vara Research.

Earlier this year, the company said it didn't renew the mandate of Paul Hudson as chief executive and appointed Belen Garijo, the boss of German peer Merck KGaA, to succeed him.

Garijo, who will step into the top role at the end of the group's annual general meeting on April 29, faces the challenge to replace sales from blockbuster Dupixent--an anti-inflammatory drug jointly developed with Regeneron--once the medicine goes off patent.

Sanofi confirmed its guidance for the current year. It continues to forecast sales to grow by a high single-digit percentage, and business earnings per share to grow slightly faster than sales, both at constant exchange rates.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Medco Energi Internasional's Earnings Set to Benefit From Some Tailwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Medco Energi Internasional's earnings stand to benefit from some tailwinds, Nomura's Ahmad Maghfur Usman says in a research report. It guides for record production of 165,000-170,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day this year, supported by factors including first full-year contribution of its Corridor block. Also, Amman Mineral should contribute earnings of $248 million following production ramp-up of its smelter. Nomura lifts its 2026-2027 earnings estimates for the Indonesian oil and gas exploration and production company by 53%-67%. It raises the stock's target price to 2,600.00 rupiah from 1,900.00 rupiah with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 2.65% higher at 1,745.00 rupiah. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)
